method: meta_tooler.py --category unreferenced cross-referenced against: periodics.json, orient.py (and orient_checks.py, orient_monitors.py, orient_sections.py, orient_analysis.py), maintenance.py, check.sh, CLAUDE.md, SWARM.md, dispatch_optimizer.py, task_order.py, plus all tools/*.py for cross-tool imports

wire.reason: Detects lesson evidence vs confidence mismatches. No callers at all. Useful quality guard.
Detects L-NNN number collisions (FM-18). Zero callers. Critical for data integrity at 1300+ lessons.
FM-27: detects unstamped/stale numerical claims. Zero callers. Prevents stale data propagation.
FM-37: detects LLM self-tagging level inflation in lessons. Zero callers. Guards science quality.
FM-38: detects false measurement instruments. Zero callers. Guards experiment validity.
FM-40: detects diagnosis-without-repair gaps. Zero callers. Guards actionability.
FM-19: detects concurrent logical overwrites at pre-commit. Zero callers despite being designed for check.sh.
Detects frontier-depleted domains. Zero callers. Prevents domain starvation.
wire.target: periodics.json (cadence ~25 sessions) or orient.py health section

wire.reason: Finds tensioned lesson pairs and proposes syntheses. Zero callers. Knowledge quality mechanism.
F-EPIS1 reliabilism gap tracker. Zero callers. Directly supports SIG-35 reliability directive.
F-EPIS1 tool reliability auditor. Only called by its own test. Supports reliability directive.
Audits HUMAN.md signal references against SIGNALS.md. Only called by test. Data integrity tool.
Synchronizes bridge files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc). Zero callers despite F118 multi-tool requirement.
Epistemological state classifier (L-707, F-META10). Referenced in MEMORY.md as key tool but not wired into automation. Called by 7 other tools.
Computes Brier scores and calibration. Zero callers. Essential for market prediction validation (finance-external directive).

archive.reason: Already has identical copy in tools/archive/eval_sufficiency.py (same SHA256). Live copy is redundant.
Already has identical copy in tools/archive/eval_sufficiency_data.py (same SHA256). Live copy is redundant.
Self-described compatibility shim that just redirects to tools/archive/testimony_calibration.py. 608 bytes. Already archived.
One-off F-SP8 experiment testing bounded/discrete fOU plateau. 12KB. Only referenced by its own test.
One-off experiment comparing fractional-OU vs mixture-of-OU. Zero callers. Statistical experiment artifact.
One-off F-SP8 experiment (discrete-native long memory INAR model). Only referenced by its own test.
One-off Hurst exponent estimator for lesson-Sharpe series. Only referenced by its own test.
One-off F-CON1 conflict baseline refresh tool. Only referenced by its own test.
One-off F-MATH8 compaction ranking comparison. Only referenced by its own test.
Models human-impact soul metric as stochastic trajectory. Only referenced by its own test. Exploratory statistics.
Dual R0 model for knowledge contagion. Zero non-self callers. Academic exploration, never integrated.
Active-inference surprise metric. Zero callers. Theoretical exploration never connected to workflow.
Rate-distortion analysis for knowledge systems. Zero callers. Information-theoretic exploration.
Unified disease scanner for knowledge systems. Zero direct automation callers. Superseded by orient_checks health diagnostics.
Classifies docs by reference footprint. Only referenced by its own test. Low utility for automation.
Tests von Neumann self-reproducing automaton conditions. Only referenced by its own test. One-off theoretical validation.
Completes domain adjacency network. Zero callers anywhere. One-time graph construction tool.
Classifies beliefs/claims by evidence source level. Zero callers. Never integrated.
Classifies lessons as prescriptive vs descriptive. Zero callers except NEXT.md mention. Low automation value.
Control theory analysis of feedback loops (F-CTL4). Zero callers. Domain experiment artifact.
Remediates PHIL-25 fairness gaps. Zero callers. fairness_audit is wired via orient_sections; enforcer is standalone and unused.

keep.reason: Called by orient_sections.py which is imported by orient.py. Core orient pipeline component. False positive in meta_tooler.
Called by orient_sections.py which is imported by orient.py. Core orient pipeline component. False positive in meta_tooler.
Imported by orient_monitors.py (build_citation_graph, analyze). Part of orient pipeline.
Imported by orient_monitors.py (_get_in_degrees). Part of orient pipeline.
Called by orient_monitors.py via subprocess. Part of orient pipeline.
Called by orient_monitors.py via subprocess. Surfaces dormant knowledge in orient output.
Shared citation parser library. Imported by citation_retrieval.py, complexity_measure.py, reach.py.
Shared lesson header parsing library. Imported by 10+ tools (brain_extractor, genesis_seeds, human_impact, knowledge_recombine, etc).
Shared reach measurement library. Imported by 16 tools (compact.py, genesis_extract.py, etc). Core infrastructure.
Lightweight multi-domain deliberation. Called by brain_extractor.py, swarm_council.py, history_integrity.py, epidemic_spread.py.
Select citation-central seed lessons. Called by genesis_extract.py. Part of reproduction pipeline.
Cross-lineage merge checker. Called by swarm_combiner.py and swarm_peer.py. Part of merge pipeline.
Mutation operator. Called by knowledge_swarm.py. Part of evolutionary mechanism.
Domain adjacency diagnostics. Called by dispatch_scoring.py.
Detect modifiable scope in foreign repos. Called by foreign_orient.py.
Goal scorers for eval_sufficiency. Called by eval_sufficiency.py (but note eval_sufficiency itself is archivable).
Extract recurring patterns from session trails. Called by orient_analysis.py and task_order_helpers.py.
Surface merge candidates in PRINCIPLES.md. Called by principle_tournament.py.
Activate council seats by scanning domains. Called by maintenance_signals.py.
PHIL-25 fairness measurement. Called by orient_sections.py. Part of orient pipeline.
ArXiv intake harness. Called by swarm_searcher.py.
Detects structural ironies. Calls knowledge_state.py and reach.py. Part of analysis toolchain.
Quality-Diversity score. Imports lesson_header.py. Standalone metric but used in knowledge assessment.

uncategorized_notes: orient_analysis, orient_monitors, citation_amplify, citation_mechanism, complexity_measure, reactivation, fairness_audit are all called via the orient.py -> orient_sections.py chain. meta_tooler flags them because it only checks direct references in the listed automation entry points, not transitive imports.
eval_sufficiency + eval_sufficiency_data have identical copies in tools/archive/ — the live copies should simply be deleted (they are the same file).

FM-series tools (stale_write_check, lesson_collision_check, false_instrument_check, diagnosis_repair_check, numerical_claim_scanner, level_inflation_check) were designed as failure-mode defenses but never wired into any automation. These are the highest-priority WIRE candidates.

Statistical experiment tools (bounded_fou, fOU_vs_mixture, fractional_inar, hurst_estimate, soul_trajectory) are one-off experiments that produced results recorded in lessons. They have no ongoing value as automation.
recommended_actions: PRIORITY 1: Wire stale_write_check into check.sh — it was designed for pre-commit but never connected (FM-19 defense gap)
PRIORITY 2: Wire lesson_collision_check into check.sh — L-NNN collisions at 1300+ lessons is a real risk
PRIORITY 3: Add FM-series tools (numerical_claim_scanner, level_inflation_check, false_instrument_check, diagnosis_repair_check) to periodics.json as a 'quality-guard' periodic at cadence ~25
PRIORITY 4: Wire sync_bridges into periodics.json — F118 multi-tool compatibility requires bridge synchronization
PRIORITY 5: Wire forecast_scorer + process_reliability + tool_reliability into periodics.json — supports SIG-35 reliability directive
PRIORITY 6: Delete tools/eval_sufficiency.py and tools/eval_sufficiency_data.py (identical to archive copies, SHA256 match confirmed)
PRIORITY 7: Archive 19 one-off experiment/exploration tools (bounded_fou, fOU_vs_mixture, fractional_inar, hurst_estimate, etc) to tools/archive/
PRIORITY 8: Fix meta_tooler.py to follow transitive imports (orient.py -> orient_sections.py -> orient_monitors.py) to reduce false positives from 70 to ~48 true unreferenced

summary.note: 12 tools (orient_analysis, orient_monitors, citation_amplify, citation_mechanism, complexity_measure, reactivation, fairness_audit, cite_parse, lesson_header, reach, trails_generalizer, gather_council) are false positives — they ARE referenced by automation but through transitive imports that meta_tooler does not follow. The remaining 58 are genuinely unreferenced by direct automation entry points, but 22 of those serve as libraries called by other tools (KEEP category).
